observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
 
a.	Create a Disposal Committee to facilitate the immediate conduct of disposal of all unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA and DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-1;

b.	Direct the GSO and Disposal Committee to submit to the COA Office the updated IIRUP with a complete list of unserviceable properties and appraisal report; and

c.	Request in writing for the disposal of the unserviceable properties to the COA Office so that inspection could be done and the LGU could maximize whatever value they may realize from its sale or disposition.

Stop immediately charges of expenditure items that are explicitly not allowed in the 20 percent Development Fund as enumerated in Section 3.2.5 of DBM, DOF, DILG JMC No.1 to ensure that only priority projects funded under the 20 percent DF that contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the LGU shall be implemented

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Engineer to submit Certificate of Completion related to the mentioned infrastructure projects to the Municipal Accountant to serve as the basis for the reclassification of completed projects from the CIP to their appropriate PPE accounts;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the cost of the completed projects immediately upon receipt of the Certificate of Completion from the Municipal Engineering Office; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to record the depreciation expenses of the PPE items starting from the date of completion.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the ₱2.372 million recorded under Construction in Progress- Buildings and Other Structures to accurately reflect the nature of the transactions and correct the overstatement of assets and government equity; and
Henceforth, strictly adhere to the criteria outlined in IPSAS 17 and Section 50 of the NGAS for LGUs when recognizing Property, Plant, and Equipment.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for the above stale checks;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee a month before the check becomes stale; and
Henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed procedures on the proper recording of stale checks in the books of the LGU.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the write-off of the long outstanding dormant account amounting to ₱234,056.67 pursuant to the specific guidelines and procedures as provided in COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the Local Economic Enterprises and 20% Development Fund, in accordance with Section 313 of Republic Act No. 7160 and the applicable provisions of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume I;
Require the Municipal Accountant to use separate subsidiary ledgers for each economic enterprise and development fund project, as prescribed; and
Ensure the preparation of complete and separate financial statements for each special account, such as Statement of Financial Position (Balance Sheet), Statement of Financial Performance (Statement of Income and Expenses), and Statement of Cash Flows and attach these to the General Fund trial balance as part of the year-end financial reporting requirements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Local Development Council (LDC) to incorporate in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) that are specifically described as to name/title, cost, and location, including concise description of the work to be done (scope and nature);
Direct the Local Sanggunian to appropriate funds only for specific programs, projects, services and activities of the Municipality; and
Secure approval or authorization from the Local Sanggunian prior to implementation of generic PPAS funded out of lump sum appropriation.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Local Development Council to adequately plan and formulate a more responsive Annual Investment Plan incorporating specific PPAs aimed to promote socio-economic and environmental developments in the Municipality; and
Ensure that Annual Investment Plan harmonizes with the Regional and Philippine Development Plan.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Discontinue the charging of prohibited expenditure items from the 20% Development Fund;
Strictly adhere to the provisions of Item 3.2.5 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the identification and implementation of eligible programs/projects for the 20 percent DF; and
Direct the Local Development Council (LDC) to keep abreast with recent circulars and guidelines to ensure proper charges against the fund.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Discontinue the charging of non-disaster-related expenditures, including General Administrative Expenses, to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF); and
Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to properly plan, identify, and prioritize specific programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) that are clearly aligned with the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to formulate and submit the required three-year GAD Agenda in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to ensure strategic, well-aligned, and measurable approach to gender mainstreaming initiatives; and
Direct the GFPS to prepare the annual GAD Plan and Budget in accordance with the GAD Agenda to ensure that identified programs and projects will answer GAD issues that directly relates to the GAD goals of the Municipality for the year.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to fully perform it mandate under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, particularly by addressing implementation delays and ensuring timely execution of GAD programs to fully realized the objectives for which the fund was allocated; and
Reassess the existing GAD Plan and Budget, and if certain Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) are deemed unnecessary, revise the plan accordingly to reallocate resources to more relevant and implementation-ready initiatives.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Local Finance Committee, in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, to revisit and conduct a comprehensive review of the Municipality’s 2017 Local Revenue Code and actively work for its update/revision in adherence to Sections 129 and 191 of the Local Government Code.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Local Economic Development and Investment Promotions Officer (LEDIPO) to prepare the mandatory 5-year Business Plan for the Local Economic Enterprise in accordance with Section 5.3 of the Manual on the Setting up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises;
Integrate the LEE’s Business Plan into the Local Development Plan of the municipality, as this will ensure proper alignment with the LGU’s overall development goals and allow for appropriate budgeting and planning at the local level; and
Direct the personnel concerned to prepare and submit the Semi-annual Financial Self-Sufficiency Review reports using the format prescribed in Annex N-2 as these reports are necessary to monitor the financial performance of the LEE, evaluate progress toward full cost recovery, and make data-driven decisions on operational adjustments.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to evaluate the LGU’s capability to undertake high-value infrastructure projects, including an assessment of the availability and adequacy of construction tools and equipment; and
Ensure that infrastructure projects are implemented by administration only when the conditions set forth under Section 3.2 (b), Appendix I of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, are fully met.

We recommended that Management:
Review and realign the existing PhilHealth Capitation Fund disposition and allocation policy (SB Resolution No. 19, S. of 2024) to ensure consistency with national regulations, particularly in prioritizing the procurement of essential medical commodities and services before payment of performance incentives; and
Strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth in PhilHealth Circular No. 2020-0022 to ensure that capitation funds are used primarily for essential health services, medicines, and operational needs as intended under the Konsulta Package Program.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the monthly subsistence allowance and hazard pay of qualified Public Health Workers and Public Social Workers based on the actual services rendered following the conditions cited in DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2012 and DBM-DSWD Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2018.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Immediately secure necessary permits and clearances from the relevant government agencies for the LGUs dredging activities in accordance with the requirements and procedures prescribed under DPWH-DENR-DILG- DOTr Joint Memorandum Circular No. 01, s. 2019;
Direct the concerned officials to prepare, maintain, and submit complete and appropriate documentation to support the dredging operations, including duly verified statements of work accomplished containing sufficient detail, as well as records on the transport and proper disposal of extracted materials; and
Ensure that all dredging activities of the LGUs comply with the established legal, environmental, safety, and regulatory standards promulgated by the relevant government agencies.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly monitor and control allocations for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses (EME) to ensure they remain within the allowable ceiling prescribed under Section 125 of R.A. No. 7160. This requirement also applies to all other budgetary accounts;
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to regularly review and reconcile budget performance reports against actual disbursements to detect and prevent overspending; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the EME and other expense accounts to support the accuracy, propriety, and classification of recorded transactions.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Consider the adoption of payroll-thru-bank scheme for the payment of salaries and wages of job order employees to minimize the attendant risks identified with cash-paid payrolls; and
Refrain from granting multiple cash advances to the same disbursing officer at the same time. Designate additional disbursing officer, if needed.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Collecting Officers to remit their collections intact daily or at the close of each business day;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collections intact daily or not later than the next banking day; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the issuance of Accountable Forms and to refrain further issuance of additional Official Receipts to collectors unless remittances of all collections are made and previous accountable forms are clearly accounted in the RAAF.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly require the submission of a certification from the Municipal Accountant to support the settlement of previous cash advance before a new cash advance is granted.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the GSO Designate to ensure that pre and post repair inspection are conducted by the duly designated property inspector of the LGU to ensure its reasonableness and necessity;
Direct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to prepare, maintain, and attach in the disbursement vouchers the record of the repair history for each equipment and motor vehicle and other documentary requirements prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001; and
Henceforth, strictly adhere to the limitations set forth in Par. 11, Annex C and Par. 2, Annex D of COA Circular 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the responsible office to immediately process the acquisition of government plates for all the motor vehicles owned by the Municipality;
Direct the concerned office to properly label with LGU name/Official Logo along with the “Official Use Only” markings for all the motor vehicles and heavy equipment owned by the Municipality; and
Strictly adhere to the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 75-6 on the use of government motor vehicles.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a.	Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Assessor, and Inventory Team Leader to furnish the Municipal Accountant copies of the TCT and Declarations of Real Property as basis of her records and valuation; and

b.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the unrecorded parcels of land based on the latest available market value and make the necessary adjusting entries to effect the correct value.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to record the depreciation of CY 2023 and henceforth, ensure the correct computation and recording of the depreciation in accordance with IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2003-007 to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the total value of the PPE in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to: 

a.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit an Activity/Program Design from Implementing Offices and approved budget for the current operating expenses before granting the Cash Advances and require pictures of the activities conducted as additional supporting documents in the liquidations; and

b.	Direct the OIC MENRO to submit accomplishment reports and documents on the data collection conducted.

We recommended and Management agreed to exert prudence in the utilization of unexpended LDRRMF and ensure that the implementation of various PPAs are aligned with the four (4) thematic areas enumerated in Section 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the BAC Secretariat to post in the PhilGEPS website the NOA, NTP and Contract Agreement for procurements conducted through public bidding. Likewise, ensure that the printed copy of the postings of NOA, NTP and Contract Agreements on the PhilGEPS website is included among the supporting documents submitted to this Office.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the OIC Engineer to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on Information and Publicity on Programs/Projects/ Activities of the Municipality, and to ensure that the posting/installation of signboards are in accordance to Annex A of the same Circular.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a.	Direct the MPDO, Municipal Engineer, LDRRMO, and all department heads/end users to ensure that all procurement requirements in the implementation of their respective programs, projects, and activities, regardless of the mode of procurement, are included in their PPMPs and submit the same to the BAC as these will be used as a basis in the preparation of APP; and

b.	Henceforth, direct the BAC and BAC Secretariat to strictly observe that no procurement shall be authorized and undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved APP, including approved changes/updates thereto.

Stop immediately charges of expenditure items that are explicitly not allowed in the 20 percent Development Fund as enumerated in Section 3.2.5 of DBM, DOF, DILG JMC No.1 to ensure that only priority projects funded under the 20 percent DF that contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the LGU shall be implemented

a.	Direct the MPDO, Municipal Engineer, LDRRMO, and all department heads/end users to ensure that all procurement requirements in the implementation of their respective programs, projects, and activities, regardless of the mode of procurement, are included in their PPMPs and submit the same to the BAC as these will be used as a basis in the preparation of APP; and

b.	Henceforth, direct the BAC and BAC Secretariat to strictly observe that no procurement shall be authorized and undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved APP, including approved changes/updates thereto.

a. Facilitate the processing of the Certificate of Title of the untitled lots in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the LGU thereof pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386;
 
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate and supervise the proper payments of expenses for the land titling and to update and reconcile the inventory of property assets with the updated records of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Assessor, and Inventory Team Leader to furnish the Municipal Accountant copies of the TCT and Declarations of Real Property as basis of her records and valuation; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the unrecorded parcels of land based on the latest available market value and make the necessary adjusting entries to effect the correct value.

3.7	We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to record the depreciation of CY 2023 and henceforth, ensure the correct computation and recording of the depreciation in accordance with IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2003-007 to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the total value of the PPE in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Create a Disposal Committee to facilitate the immediate conduct of disposal of all unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA and DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-1;
Direct the GSO and Disposal Committee to submit to the COA Office the updated IIRUP with a complete list of unserviceable properties and appraisal report; and
Request in writing for the disposal of the unserviceable properties to the COA Office so that inspection could be done and the LGU could maximize whatever value they may realize from its sale or disposition.

7.8      We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit an Activity/Program Design from Implementing Offices and approved budget for the current operating expenses before granting the Cash Advances and require pictures of the activities conducted as additional supporting documents in the liquidations; and
Direct the OIC MENRO to submit accomplishment reports and documents on the data collection conducted.

9.7	We recommended and Management agreed to exert prudence in the utilization of unexpended LDRRMF and ensure that the implementation of various PPAs are aligned with the four (4) thematic areas enumerated in Section 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

10.5 	We recommended and Management agreed to direct the BAC Secretariat to post in the PhilGEPS website the NOA, NTP and Contract Agreement for procurements conducted through public bidding. Likewise, ensure that the printed copy of the postings of NOA, NTP and Contract Agreements on the PhilGEPS website is included among the supporting documents submitted to this Office.

11.5	We recommended and Management agreed to direct the OIC Engineer to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on Information and Publicity on Programs/Projects/Activities of the Municipality, and to ensure that the posting/installation of signboards are in accordance to Annex A of the same Circular.

a.	Stop the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than for official traveling expenses as provided in Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular 97-002.

b.	Refrain procurement of goods through cash advances and ensure that the purchases of routinary items like materials and supplies necessary in the conduct of various projects/programs/activities of the LGU conforms to the provisions of RA 9184 also known as the Government Procurement Reform Act.

a.	Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit timely the submission of the financial reports, records of receipts and disbursements, and bank reconciliation statements to facilitate an auditorial review; and

b.	Henceforth, strictly comply with the aforementioned laws, rules, and regulations.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

   a. Instruct the General Services Officer-Designate to  maintain  property
      cards following the prescribed procedures in  property  recording  and
      maintain an updated, accurate and complete property records;


   b. Direct the GSO-Designate to submit an updated RPCPPE  as  of  December
      31, 2021;

   c.  Assign  a  full-time  Supply/Property  Officer,  if   possible,   and
      reconstitute the Inventory Committee and  prepare  Physical  Inventory
      Plan (PIP) specifying the  specific  assignments,  the  cut-off  date,
      specifying  the  schedule  dates  and  location  of  inventory  taking
      activities from start to the targeted date of completion  as  provided
      in section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 2020-006;

   d. Direct the Municipal  Accountant  and  the  GSO-Designate  to  conduct
      reconciliation of PPE by  category  and  its  PPELC  and  PC  records,
      respectively and prepare adjustment in the books of accounts  and  the
      property records, respectively; and

   e. Require the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to comply
      with the guidelines and procedures set forth in the COA  Circular  No.
      2020-006 to address  issues  and  concerns  on  the  non-existing  and
      unserviceable assets.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
   a. Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat  together  with
      the Accountant  to  immediately  submit  all  the  lacking  supporting
      documents enumerated in Annex I to enable the Audit Team  to  evaluate
      the procurements and strictly adhere to subsections of  Section  9  of
      COA Circular No. 2012-001;


   b. Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to use  the  required  format  as
      provided in the GPPB Circular No. 04-2020 dated September 16, 2020;


   c. Direct the BAC  Secretariat  to  submit  contracts  and  its  attached
      documents to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days in
      adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001  and  Section
      9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001;

   d. Direct  the  Accountant  to  ensure  proper  and  complete  supporting
      documents are attached to all disbursement vouchers;

   e. Direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure filling up of complete data in
      the POs/contracts together with Notice of Award and Notice to  Proceed
      indicating the date of receipt by supplier/contractor  signifying  his
      conformity to the terms and conditions of the POs/contracts; and

   f. Direct the Acceptance and Inspection  Committee  to  indicate  in  the
      Acceptance and Inspection Report the actual  date  of  acceptance  and
      inspection of deliveries.

We recommend and the management agreed to:


   a. Direct the LDRRM Officer to prepare and submit each month a Report  on
      Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the audit team on  or  before  the
      15th day after the end of each month; and


   b. Submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for CY  2021  in
      required format without delay.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

   a. Direct the GSO-Designate and the Accountant to maintain a  repair  and
      maintenance record/history  of  each  equipment/vehicle  owned  and/or
      operated by the Municipality to track down the frequency of repair and
      to avoid unnecessary repair and maintenance expenses;

   b. Direct the GSO-Designate and the Accountant to attach the  repair  and
      maintenance history/record to  the  disbursement  vouchers  for  major
      repairs and maintenance expenses as supporting documents; and


   c. Direct the GSO-Designate and the  Accountant  to  determine  if  major
      repairs can add up the life of the motor vehicles  and  recompute  the
      depreciation expense to the remaining life of the asset.

We recommended and the management agreed to:


   a. Direct the Municipal Health Officer and/or concerned RHU personnel  to
      furnished the Municipal Accountant, the documents necessary to  record
      the receipt and distribution of  vaccines,  ancillaries  and  supplies
      Inventory Reports or its equivalent in  relation  to  the  vaccination
      program; and


   b.  Direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  record  using  the  perpetual
      inventory system,  the  vaccines  received  and  its  distribution  as
      reported by the LGU to DOH-CHD; and draw journal entry voucher for the
      applicable  inventory  accounts  to  record  the  remaining  vaccines,
      ancillaries and supplies in stock at December 31, 2021.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to immediately submit the aforementioned supporting documents to enable the Audit Team to evaluate the procurements of the ₱6.678 million;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to throughly verify each disbursement as to the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents to strengthen internal control in the disbursement process; and
Adhere to the procedures and requirements provided in Section 4 (6) of PD 1445, Item 9.2.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 particularly in the conduct of emergency procurement under the Bayanihan Act.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant;

observations.managementAction: Designated an officer-in-charged for the titling of the lot under the supervision of the Municipal Mayor. The Management will resurvey the untitled lots.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Strictly implement all projects, programs and activities of its mandatory funds in relation to its Annual Budget;
Require the GAD Focal Person and the LDRRM Officer that during identification of PPA’s for budgeting, include only those which are feasible and can be completed within the target date of implementation, taking into consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership; and
See to it that PPAs being implemented are closely monitored and supervised so that problems/issues that may arise during the implementation shall be immediately addressed.

Facilitate the processing of Certificate of Title of the untitled lots in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the LGU thereof pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386; Direct the Municipal Assessor to appraise the untitled lots to its current market value and provide the same to the Municipal Accountant for reference in recording in the book of accounts; Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the subject property assets upon securing the Certificate of Title using the actual costs or appraised value if the former could not be determined; and Direct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate and supervise the proper payments of expenses for the land titling and to update and reconcile the inventory of property assets with the updated records of the Municipal Accountant.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:


 a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the duly certified
    list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due  and  collectible
    for the year to the Municipal Accountant;




 b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to set-up RPT/SET Receivables at  the
    beginning of the year and make necessary adjusting entries  to  reflect
    the correct income for the reporting period; and




 c.  Adopt  the Accrual  Method of accounting real  property  taxes  in
    accordance with Paragraph 1 of IPSAS  and  Section  246  of  the  Local
    Government Code of 1991.

a. Facilitate the processing of Certificate of Title  of  the
    untitled lots in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest
    of the LGU thereof pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386;


 b. Direct the Municipal Assessor to appraise  the  untitled  lots  to  its
    current market value and provide the same to the  Municipal  Accountant
    for reference in recording in the book of accounts;


 c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the subject  property  assets
    upon securing the Certificate  of  Title  using  the  actual  costs  or
    appraised value if the former could not be determined; and


 d. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate and supervise  the  proper
    payments of expenses for the land titling and to update  and  reconcile
    the inventory of property  assets  with  the  updated  records  of  the
    Municipal Accountant.

a. Strictly  implement  all  projects,  programs  and  activities  of  its
    mandatory funds in relation to its Annual Budget;


 b. Require the  GAD  Focal  Person  and  the  LDRRM  Officer  that  during
    identification of PPA’s for budgeting, include  only  those  which  are
    feasible and can be completed within the target date of implementation,
    taking into consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership; and


 c. See to it  that  PPAs  being  implemented  are  closely  monitored  and
    supervised  so  that  problems/issues  that  may   arise   during   the
    implementation shall be immediately addressed.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management: Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials to initiate massive tax campaign for efficient and aggressive tax collection; Promptly enforce the remedies provided under the following Sections of the Code in order to intensify the collection of delinquent RPT: Section 256 – Application of remedies by administrative action through levy on real property or by judicial action; and Section 259 – Imposition of penalties for failure to issue and execute warrant of levy; and Seek assistance from the 42 barangays in the Municipality for the implementation of municipality’s tax campaign program, particularly in the dissemination of notices of delinquency and serving the warrants of levy to the delinquent real property owners within their territorial jurisdiction.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management: Formulate the annual GAD Plan within the context of the mandate and overall plans and programs geared towards the protection of women’s and children rights against gender-based violence considering the vulnerability of different age brackets; Develop comprehensive strategies and policies to provide and enable access to a full range of high-quality health care services as assistance for gender-based violence victims towards healing, recovery and empowerment; Establish linkages with NGAs that advocates the protection of women’s and children rights and sought their participation to appropriate GAD activities being implemented; Earmark funding for the establishment of Crises Center that will assist and protect victims of gender-based violence in the litigation of their cases and their recovery; Strictly implement the identified GAD projects, programs and activities in relation to its Annual Budget; and Evaluate the GAD PPAs implemented for prevention, intervention and reintegration in addressing gender related issues and concerns in terms of benefits delivered to target beneficiaries.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management initiate the adjudication of the eight non-operational market stalls and strictly enforce the provisions of Section 54 and 57 of the Economic Enterprise Code to maximize the utilization of public market with the end view of generating more income for the LGU.

The Committee on Awards shall effect the applicable mode of disposal, proper documentation, adequate publicity and notification for Public Auction “As Is Where Is” as soon as possible to avoid further deterioration of the properties;

Keep/maintain basic record on the operation of SWM required under Section 42 of RA 9003 and keep track of the current condition of the dumping site to promote proper monitoring of the program and to facilitate compliance to Rule VII Section 7 of the same RA;
Initiate the creation and maintenance of GAD Database containing sex-disaggregated data pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01;
such as the installation of a workable drainage system/flood controls;

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials to initiate massive tax
campaign for efficient and aggressive tax collection;

b. Promptly enforce the remedies provided under the following Sections of the Code in
order to intensify the collection of delinquent RPT:
Section 256 – Application of remedies by administrative action through levy on real
property or by judicial action; and
Section 259 – Imposition of penalties for failure to issue and execute warrant of levy; and

c. Seek assistance from the 42 barangays in the Municipality for the implementation of
municipality’s tax campaign program, particularly in the dissemination of notices of
delinquency and serving the warrants of levy to the delinquent real property owners
within their territorial jurisdiction.

The audit finding, referred to above, was earlier communicated to management through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2019-01 dated January 14, 2019.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:

a. Formulate the annual GAD Plan within the context of the mandate and overall plans
and programs geared towards the protection of women’s and children rights against
gender-based violence considering the vulnerability of different age brackets;

b. Develop comprehensive strategies and policies to provide and enable access to a
full range of high quality health care services as assistance for gender-based
violence victims towards healing, recovery and empowerment;

c. Establish linkages with NGAs that advocates the protection of women’s and children
rights and sought their participation to appropriate GAD activities being implemented;

d. Earmark funding for the establishment of Crises Center that will assist and protect
victims of gender-based violence in the litigation of their cases and their recovery;

e. Strictly implement the identified GAD projects, programs and activities in relation to
its Annual Budget; and

f. Evaluate the GAD PPAs implemented for prevention, intervention and reintegration
in addressing gender related issues and concerns in terms of benefits delivered to
target beneficiaries.

The audit finding, referred to above, was earlier communicated to management through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2019-02 dated January 18, 2019.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management initiate the adjudication of
the eight non-operational market stalls and strictly enforce the provisions of Section 54
and 57 of the Economic Enterprise Code to maximize the utilization of public market
with the end view of generating more income for the LGU.

The audit finding, referred to above, was earlier communicated to management through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2019-04 dated February 13, 2019.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:

a. Facilitate the processing of Certificate of Title of the untitled lots in order to establish
absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the LGU thereof pursuant to
Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386;

b. Direct the Municipal Assessor to appraise the untitled lots to its current market value
and provide the same to the Municipal Accountant for reference in recording in the
book of accounts;

c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the subject property assets upon securing
the Certificate of Title using the actual costs or appraised value if the former could not
be determined; and

d. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate and supervise the proper payments of
expenses for the land titling and to update and reconcile the inventory of property
assets with the updated records of the Municipal Accountant.

The audit finding, referred to above, was earlier communicated to management through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2019-05 dated February 14, 2019.
Create an Appraisal Committee headed by the Municipal Assessor and representatives from each department concerned;
Direct the Appraisal Committee to conduct the appraisal of the unserviceable properties and submit the same to the designated disposal committee for reference
Designate a Committee on Awards to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties;

Direct the Designated Property Custodian to immediately prepare Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) as well as the corrected RPCPPE to be submitted to the Office of the Audit Team Leader and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant;

Reconcile the accounting records against the corrected RPCPPE as to the remaining existing properties of the LGU and reclassify those PPE that has already reached its salvage value to “Other Asset” account; and
Draw journal entry vouchers to derecognize the unserviceable properties and apply appropriate accounting procedures to effect adjustments in the books.

Require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and the General Services Officer to comply to the Accounting and Reporting Procedures and to their respective duties and responsibilities provided in Items V and VII of COA Circular No. 2017-008 dated November 23, 2017, respectively;
Submit a duly accomplished copy of the Annexes A to E of COA Circular No. 2017-008 to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification;

Direct the Municipal Engineer to prepare specific estimated useful life for each depreciable components of the Local Road Network based on LGU’s experience on the life of its PPE, copy furnished the Audit Team;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the corresponding depreciation and make necessary disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements;

In the existing Inventory Committee, the inclusion of a representative from the Engineering Department is recommended to facilitate identification and evaluation of the technical aspect of the projects.
On the compliance of Rule X Section 1 of the IRR of R.A. 9003
Set limitation on the use of the dump truck outside its main purpose to avoid frequent maintenance of the vehicle brought by constant use and the possible health problems that may arise;

Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan be reviewed with budget allocation for optimum implementation of identified PPAs and observe limitation on the types of PPAs that may be funded under the said fund to adhere the requirements laid down under the RA 9003;
Encourage private organizations and non-government organizations to join the LGU in its effort of keeping its environment clean, healthy and green.
Conduct monitoring of the leachate contamination of ground water and surface water in accordance with Rule XIII Section 2 (b) of the IRR of R.A. 9003;
Earmark funding for the provision of gate/hinge barrier for the MRF/Disposal Site to provide complete enclosure and avoid unauthorized entries of individuals and animals;

Require the MSWDO that during identification of PPA’s for budgeting, propose only those which are feasible and can be completed within the target date of implementation, taking into consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership;
Strictly implement all LCPC projects, programs and activities in relation to its Annual Budget;
See to it that PPAs being implemented are closely monitored and supervised so that problems/issues that may arise shall be immediately addressed;
Include the end-result of the activities undertaken in the Accomplishment Report;
Encourage private organizations and non-government organizations to join the LGU in its effort of upholding the rights of children.

Establish GAD agenda or identify priority gender issues using the results of gender analysis and assessment provided by sex-disaggregated data as basis for the formulation of PPAs to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget;
Identify and include appropriate PPAs that address priority gender issues in the GPB;
Identify specific gender indicators that would help keep track progress made for purposes of consistency and sustainability of the identified GAD-focused PPAs;

Determine efficiency and effectiveness of interventions in addressing gender related issues and concerns taking into account the results of PPAs evaluation in terms of benefits to target beneficiaries;

Review the GBP to determine if the identified PPAs address the gender issues and concerns of constituents and employees and accomplishment reports to identify remaining gender issues that have not been addressed.

Require the MSWDO that during identification of PPAs for budgeting, propose only those which are feasible and can be completed within the target date of implementation, taking into consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership;
See to it that PPAs being implemented are closely monitored and supervised so that problems/issues that may arise shall be immediately addressed;
Include the end-result of the activities undertaken in the Accomplishment Report;

Direct the Municipal Engineering and Municipal Planning and Development Offices to take measures that would totally resolve the year round flash flooding to low land barangays through the adoption of engineering techniques and hazard-resistant such as the installation of a workable drainage system/flood controls;
Identify relocation site for residents living along the river/creeks which are considered danger zones; and

Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities identified as priorities particularly on its Prevention and Mitigation aspects to limit or avoid potential adverse impacts of hazard and related disasters.

Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, wages, allowances, etc. by opening of individual ATM savings accounts through a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with any of the government servicing bank stationed in Roxas City; and
Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the MOA and make representations for possible establishment of the applicable type of ATM to the premises of the LGU.

It was recommended that the LGU should create an Appraisal Committee led by the
Municipal Assessor to appraise the said properties to its current market value for the
Municipal Accountant to record the same in the books in lieu of its unidentified
acquisition costs. The reconciliation of the accounting records with the actual land
owned by the LGU is necessary to ascertain the validity, existence and accuracy of such
account shown in the Financial Statements. All noted differences as a result of
comparison of both records (Property and Accounting) shall be summarized, cleared and
settled immediately. Thus, the latter should coordinate with the Appraisal Committee for
proper identification of unrecorded lands owned by and/or donated to the LGU, if any.
Consequently, Deeds of Donation and other supporting documents for donated lands
should be secured and make the necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial
Statements.
Unserviceable/Obsolete properties with a book value of ₱520,520.62 were still
included in the General Ledger under Other Property, Plant and Equipment
Account and appropriate disposal of such properties has not yet been undertaken
contrary to COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989, Section 79 of
Presidential Decree (PD) 1445 and Article 435 of the Implementing Rules and
Regulations (IRR) of the Local Government Code of 1991 hence, would entail
further deterioration and loss of additional income that could have accrued to the
LGU had they been disposed of.
Section 79 of PD 1445 states that:
“When government property has become unserviceable for any cause, or is
no longer needed, it shall, upon application of the officer accountable
therefore, be inspected by the head of the agency or his duly authorized
representative in the presence of the auditor concerned and, if found to be
valueless or unsalable, it may be destroyed in their presence. If found to be
valuable, it may be sold at public auction to the highest bidder under the
supervision of the proper committee on award or similar body in the presence
of the auditor concerned or other duly authorized representative of the
Commission. Xxxx”
Moreover, COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989 provides:
“The full and sole authority and responsibility for the divestment or disposal of
property and other assets owned by the national government agencies or
instrumentalities, local government units, and government-owned and/or
controlled corporations and their subsidiaries shall be lodged in the heads of
the departments, bureaus, and offices of the national government, the local
government units, and the governing bodies or managing heads of
government-owned or controlled corporations and their subsidiaries
conformably to their respective corporate charters or articles of incorporation,
who shall constitute the appropriate committee or body to undertake the
same.”
The Audit Team conducted an inspection on November 23, 2017 of the unserviceable
properties to validate its existence. However, the completeness of such properties
cannot be ascertained due to the non-submission of Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) by the LGU. Moreover, the LGU was not able to
substantiate its accuracy due to lack of details and relevant information.
Ocular Inspection revealed that the unserviceable properties of the LGU include
equipment, metal cabinets and some machines like photocopier machines. However,
there was no information relayed to the team as to the year they became unserviceable
and the corresponding action taken by the management, if any. As reflected in the
LGU’s Financial Position as of December 31, 2017, the total book value of these
properties represents salvage value. Inspection also revealed that most of these
properties remained in their offices occupying large spaces of working area. Shown
below are some of the pictures of unserviceable properties taken during the inspection.
Several Unserviceable/Obsolete Properties of the LGU as of C.Y 2017
In this regard, Article 435 of the IRR of the Local Government Code of 1991 mandates
that there shall be in every province, city, or municipality a Committee on Awards for the
disposal of unserviceable properties which shall exercise exclusive jurisdiction in
deciding the winning bids and questions of awards on the disposal of supplies and
properties except in the cases of procurement through emergency purchase, or when
the amortization is specifically vested by law in another body. For the municipality, the
Committee on Awards shall be composed of the Local Chief Executive as Chairman, the
Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Budget Officer, and the
head of office or department of whose supplies are being procured, as members. These
properties should be appraised by in-house or by an independent appraiser so that the
government shall receive fair compensation for such properties.
Accordingly, the same COA Circular provides that the Commission as stipulated by
Article 444 of the IRR of Local Government Code, recognizes the following modes of
disposal/divestment of assets and properties of national government agencies, local
government units and government-owned or controlled corporation and their
subsidiaries, aside from other such modes as may be provided for by law: (a) Public
Bidding; (b) Negotiation; (c) Barter, etc.
It is to be noted that in all modes or instances of disposal of government property or
assets, the proceedings shall be undertaken by the appropriate authority in the presence
of the Auditor or the COA representative who shall act as a witness thereto.
The unserviceable assets do not contribute economic benefits to the LGU unless
disposed for consideration. Thus, the proceeds from the sale of the unserviceable
properties shall be receipted by the Municipal Treasurer and recorded by the Municipal
Accountant and thereafter make necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that management:
For the Appraisal Committee:
1. Create an Appraisal Committee headed by the Municipal Assessor and
representatives from each department concerned;
2. Direct the Appraisal Committee to conduct the appraisal of the unserviceable
properties and submit the same to the designated disposal committee for reference;
For the Committee on Awards:
3. Designate a Committee on Awards to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable
properties;
4. The Committee on Awards shall effect the applicable mode of disposal, proper
documentation, adequate publicity and notification for Public Auction “As Is Where Is”
as soon as possible to avoid further deterioration of the properties;
5. Direct the Designated Property Custodian to immediately prepare Inventory and
Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) as well as the corrected
RPCPPE to be submitted to the Office of the Audit Team Leader and furnish the
same to the Municipal Accountant;
For the Municipal Accountant:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
6. Reconcile the accounting records against the corrected RPCPPE as to the remaining
existing properties of the LGU and reclassify those PPE that has already reached its
salvage value to “Other Asset” account; and
7. Draw journal entry vouchers to derecognize the unserviceable properties and apply
appropriate accounting procedures to effect adjustments in the books.
Management Comment:
The Management acknowledge that there are indeed some untitled lands claimed as
own by the LGU. As a result, it was not recorded in the books of accounts due to the
absence of the documents of ownership. There are Tax Declarations present in the
Municipal Assessor’s Office however this document does not necessarily serves as an
evidence of ownership. On the other hand, Management answered that the creation of
Committee on Awards will be made as soon as possible to facilitate the disposal of the
unserviceable properties. Moreover, the Property Custodian said that the IIRUP will be
made based on the existing unserviceable properties to facilitate the proper disposal of
the unserviceable properties and to correct the RPCPPE thereafter.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
1) Require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and the General Services
Officer to comply to the Accounting and Reporting Procedures and to their respective
duties and responsibilities provided in Items V and VII of COA Circular No. 2017-008
dated November 23, 2017, respectively;
2) Submit a duly accomplished copy of the Annexes A to E of COA Circular
No. 2017-008 to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification;
3) Direct the Municipal Engineer to prepare specific estimated useful life for each
depreciable components of the Local Road Network based on LGU’s experience on
the life of its PPE, copy furnished the Audit Team;
4) Direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the corresponding depreciation and make
necessary disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and
5) In the existing Inventory Committee, the inclusion of a representative from the
Engineering Department is recommended to facilitate identification and evaluation of
the technical aspect of the projects.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Engineer stated during the Exit Conference that the Municipal
Engineering Office is preparing the Local Road Network of the Municipality with the
corresponding class and a copy will be furnished to the Audit Team when completed.
Moreover, she added that they will furnish the Municipal Accountant the useful life of
each of the depreciable component of Road and Road Network System based on the
prescribed useful life provided by the concerned National Government Agencies.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to cause the timely preparation and submission of BRS together with the Bank Statements, credit/debit memos and paid checks for five(5) depository accounts within the prescribed period provided in COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the journal entry voucher/s duly supported with documents showing the adjustments noted during the audit.

For delayed Submission of DV, FS, Status of Unliquidated Cash advances, Report on the Information and Publicity on Programs/Projects
Promptly submit to the Audit Team the FS, reports, disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents, etc. to facilitate the complete review and analysis of accounts to affirm the reliability of the management’s assertions on the existence/occurrence, validity, accuracy and completeness of the recorded transactions.
For Non-submission of RPCPPE
Conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of PPE and reconcile the results thereof with the accounting records; Maintain the PPE Ledger Card for all group/class of PPE; Require the Supply Officer or Designated Official to complete and submit the RPCPPE with all information as to date of acquisition, cost of assets, etc., to the Accountant; and
For Non-submission of GAD Accomplishment Report
Identify priority gender issues using the results of gender analysis and assessment as basis of formulating PPAs in GAD Plan and Budget; Identify specific gender indicators that would help keep tracl progress made for consistency and sustainability of the identified GAD-focused PPAs; and
Review audit findings and recommendations to determine the remaining gender issues not addressed by the GAD Plan and Budget.
For Non-submission of Report and Utilization of DRRMF
Require the MDRRMO to submit the aforesaid report every 15th day of each month
For Non-submission of Monthly Bank Reconciliations on all bank accounts
Verify the abnormal cash balances, reconcile the discrepancies of the book and bank balances and effect the correcting/adjusting entries for the discrepancies/errors noted.
Request monthly bank statements and prepare Monthly BRS on all bank accounts.  In cases of delayed receipt of bank statements, make representations with the concerned depository banks for prompt submission of bank statements.
For Non-submission of copies of contracts and purchase orders with supporting documents
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and Secretary to furnish the Audit Team with copies of the aforesaid documents within five (5) days after perfection.

Direct the BAC secretariat to furnish the Audit Team with perfected contracts awarded for the period June 2015 to July 2016 to evaluate whether the claim of honorarium is valid and within the limits prescribed under DBM Budget Circular 2004-5 and 5A; Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the complete attachments of successfully completed procurement projects or contract prior to payment of honoraria to chair and members of the BAC and TWG, and overtime to government personnel involved in the procurement process and submit the same within the five (5) working days from the execution of a contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12,2009; and
Require the BAC Secretariat to maintain a log book containing all relevant information on the procurement process up to the award of contract in a systematic way so as to provide bases for granting honorarium and overtime to personnel involve in procurement activities.

Direct the Municipal Assessor to establish proper coordination and linkages with other heads of departments i.e., Treasury and Accounting or with other Local and National Government Agencies i.e., Province, Register of Deeds and DAR among others, to obtain/request assistance/information/ data/documents needed to appraise real property to its current value or to reflect the prevailing market prices of properties for RPT purposes and submit the AR to the Municipal Treasurer for the preparation of Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card; Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and
Based on the list, direct the Municipal Accountant shall draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the debit to RPT/SET Receivables and crediting to Deferred RPT Income/Deferred SET Income for proper recording of Receivables and Deferred Income at the beginning of the year.

Take appropriate action to refund the amount of P180,745.00 and return or immediately remit the unutilized balance of ₱362,050.00 to the Bureau of Treasury, in compliance with the Supreme Court Decision (EN BANC) dated November 19, 2013; and
Submit the Journal and Adjusting entries as a result of the above transactions.

Design and submit a Flow Chart documenting the fiscal control process from the approval of claims up to the payment to laborers as demanded by the sound internal control thereby ensuring that all resources are expended or utilized in accordance with law and regulations and safeguarded against loss or wastage through illegal or improper disposition; and
Ensure faithful adherence to the aforesaid Flow Chart designed to further facilitate or expedite government transactions with a view to ensuring efficiency and effectiveness in the payment of labor claims; and
Ensure that when a cash advance is drawn for labor purposes, it shall be appended with a summary of payrolls of the different programs/projects/activities for the period.  The amount of cash advance offsets all of the labor expenses paid by the disbursing officer who then submits the liquidation report to reconcile two records, during the period.

Establish GAD agenda or identify priority gender issues using the results of gender analysis and assessment provided by sex-disaggregated data as basis for the formulation of PPAs to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget; Identify and include appropriate PPAs that address priority gender issues in the GPB; Identify specific gender indicators that would help keep track progress made for purposes of consistency and sustainability of the identified GAD-focused PPAs; Determine efficiency and effectiveness of interventions in addressing gender related issues and concerns taking into account the results of PPAs evaluation in terms of benefits to target beneficiaries; and
Review the GBP to determine if the identified PPAs address the gender issues and concerns of constituents and employees and accomplishment reports to identify remaining gender issues that have not been addressed.

Earmark funding support for “Research Study” with the end view of collecting evidence to determine the appropriate programs, projects and activities for a more equitable and efficient allocation of SEF budget; Direct all concerned DepEd officials, teachers and school heads who are recipient of financial assistance in connection with their travel, conduct of various sports and school activities like “Brigada Eskwela”, and minor building repairs to submit appropriate documents with a view of determining whether the financial assistance was utilized for its intended purpose/s; Submit Program of Work duly approved by the Local School Board for the construction/repair of school buildings, classrooms and facilities Implemented by Administration; and
Submit certification from the School Head that the payment of utilities (Water, Electric and Telephone Expenses) are not included in the SIP as approved by the DepEd Superintendent for the current year.

Prioritize the implementation of the identified PPAs based on the LDRRM Plan and monitor PPAs outcomes to ensure that the impact of calamities to peoples’ lives and damage to properties are minimized, if not, totally eliminated; Submit justification why the LDRRM focused-PPAs that could best addressed the purposes of Disaster Preparedness and Mitigation to reduce future casualties and property damage are not Implemented; and
Consider in the planning phase the construction choices of projects funded out of LDRRM fund for long-lasting structures so as to enhance its disaster preparedness and avoid mass casualties and destruction of properties, if possible, establish linkages for other funding opportunities.

On the condition of MRF/dumpsite:
Earmark fund for the completion of the MRF subject to the guidelines and criteria set pursuant to RA 9003 for final sorting, segregation, composting and recycling; On utilization of SWM Fund:
Observe the limitations on the types of PPAs that may be funded under the SWM Fund to adhere the requirements laid down under the RA 9003 and realign PPAs that should be charged under LDRRM Fund.
On waste segregation:
Direct the SWM Task Force to intensify its information campaign so that concerned residents shall ensure that solid wastes are properly segregated prior to collection considering the different identification of solid waste as "compostable", "non-recyclable", "recyclable" and "special waste" or “hazardous waste”; On record keeping:
Keep/maintain basic record on the operation of SWM required under Section 42 of RA 9003 and keep track of the current condition of the dumping site to promote proper monitoring of the program.

Require the necessary approval from local sanggunian prior to the implementation of the above projects in the total amount of ₱44.194 million in compliance with the provision of the law and following the ruling provided in the afore-cited jurisprudence; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to revert to unappropriated surplus of the GF, the unobligated balances of continuing appropriation from CY 2012-2015 as of December 31, 2016 in accordance with the COA Circular No. 89-302 dated December 29, 1989.

Request the Invoice Receipt of Property (IRP) from the donor-agency (BFAR and NFA) containing the total value of the non-cash donations for the following purposes: 1) issuance of Acknowledgement Receipt; 2) inclusion as one of the sources of LDRRM Fund; and  3) complete and accurate recording in the books of LGU; Furnish copies of the IRP to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the LGU books using the account “Trust Liabilities-LDRRM”; Furnish evidence of distribution duly acknowledged by the identified beneficiaries with valid identification such as BIR Form 0016 (Residence Certificate) and photos for proper identification for 900 bags of rice from the National Food Authority (NFA); Require the beneficiaries to sign the Acknowledgement Receipt of Property (ARE) for the 24 units fiberglass boat received from the Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources (BFAR); and Henceforth, comply strictly the requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the submission of Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

Comply strictly to the terms and conditions required in the utilization of BFAR funds and ensure that all necessary supporting documents, such as, list of beneficiaries, location, pictures, etc., are submitted to the concerned agencies; Henceforth, submit monthly status reports and documents within ten (10) days after the end of every quarter for every trust fund received from other government and non-government agencies; Ensure that the total amount of P4.074 million was utilized solely on the victims of Typhoon Yolanda and to areas declared as heavily affected so as to alleviate the economic and social conditions of the identified beneficiaries; and
Release immediately any unutilized amount to augment the pressing needs of the beneficiaries.

Submit the identified location or sites of BFAR projects including photos for target planting activities and actual seedling/planting activities (with geo-tagging photos); Furnish copies of the Progress Report, Statement of Work Accomplished based on the Project Proposal submitted to the BFAR; and
Submit List of beneficiaries with valid identification such as BIR Form 0016 and photos for proper identification.

Secure all relevant documents or any information on LGU rights to recover the value of invested capital whether written or unwritten or through collective memory and the use of witnesses; Request for write-off of Other Investment account that were considered dormant for over five years and beyond recovery from the Commission on Audit duly supported with proper documentation, pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997.

Secure all documents or any information on LGU rights over the subject land whether written or unwritten or through collective memory and the use of witnesses; Implement the titling procedures which may include land surveying and land valuation including taxation with the assistance of land administration agencies; and Submit proper documentation as evidenced of land ownership; otherwise, stop any construction and improvement on the untitled land for lack of recognition and protection under the law.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to cause the timely preparation and submission of BRS together with the Bank SFtatements, credit/debit memos and paid checks for five (5) depository accounts within the prescribed period provided in COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the journal entry voucher/s duly supported with documents showing the adjustments noted during the audit.

For delayed Submission of DV, FS, Status of Unliquidated Cash advances, Report on the Information and Publicity on Programs/Projects
Promptly submit to the Audit Team the FS, reports, disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents, etc. to facilitate the complete review and analysis of accounts to affirm the reliability of the management’s assertions on the existence/occurrence, validity, accuracy and completeness of the recorded transactions.

Conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of PPE and reconcile the results thereof with the accounting records;
Maintain the PPE Ledger Card for all group/class of PPE;
Require the Supply Officer or Designated Official to complete and submit the RPCPPE with all information as to date of acquisition, cost of assets, etc., to the Accountant so that the latter can compute and recognize depreciation charges for all PPE accounts;
For Non-submission of GAD Accomplishment Report
Identify  priority gender issues using the results of gender analysis and assessment as basis of formulating PPAs in GAD Plan and Budget;
Identify specific gender indicators that would help keep tracl progress made for consistency and sustainability of the identified GAD-focused PPAs; and
Review audit findings and recommendations to determine the remaining gender issues not addressed by the GAD Plan and Budget.
For Non-submission of Report and Utilization of DRRMF
Require the MDRRMO to submit the aforesaid report every 15th day of each month
For Non-submission of Monthly Bank Reconciliations on all bank accounts
Verify the abnormal cash balances, reconcile the discrepancies of the book and bank balances and effect the correcting/adjusting entries for the discrepancies/errors noted.
Request monthly bank statements and prepare Monthly BRS on all bank accounts.  In cases of delayed receipt of bank statements, make representations with the concerned depository banks for prompt submission of bank statements
For Non-submission of copies of contracts and purchase orders with supporting documents
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and Secretary to furnish the Audit Team with copies of the aforesaid documents within five (5) days after perfection.

Take appropriate action to refund the amount of P180,745.00 and return or immediately remit the unutilized balance of ₱362,050.00 to the Bureau of Treasury, in compliance with the Supreme Court Decision (EN BANC) dated November 19, 2013; and
Submit the Journal and Adjusting entries as a result of the above transactions.

Design and submit a Flow Chart documenting the fiscal control process from the approval of claims up to the payment to laborers as demanded by the sound internal control thereby ensuring that all resources are expended or utilized in accordance with law and regulations and safeguarded against loss or wastage through illegal or improper disposition; and
Ensure faithful adherence to the aforesaid Flow Chart designed to further facilitate or expedite government transactions with a view to ensuring efficiency and effectiveness in the payment of labor claims; and
Ensure that when a cash advance is drawn for labor purposes, it shall be appended with a summary of payrolls of the different programs/projects/activities for the period.  The amount of cash advance offsets all of the labor expenses paid by the disbursing officer who then submits the liquidation report to reconcile two records, during the period.

Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, wages, allowances, etc. by opening of individual ATM savings accounts through a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with any of the government servicing bank stationed in Roxas City; and
Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the MOA and make representations for possible establishment of the applicable type of ATM to the premises of the LGU.

Require the necessary approval from local sanggunian prior to the implementation of the above projects in the total amount of ₱44.194 million in compliance with the provision of the law and following the ruling provided in the afore-cited jurisprudence; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to revert to unappropriated surplus of the GF, the unobligated balances of continuing appropriation from CY 2012-2015 as of December 31, 2016 in accordance with the COA Circular No. 89-302 dated December 29, 1989.

Require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate all remaining Purchase Requests submitted by recipient barangays as basis of bulk procurement using the primary mode of procurement; Observe strictly the accounting procedure in recording accumulated cost of construction materials acquired for stock subject to issuance for the construction of project undertaken by administration; Use the appropriate account code of other cost component upon acquisition in accordance with the existing COA rules and regulations; Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the Subsidiary Ledger for each fund received from National Government Agencies for implementation of specific programs and projects indicating, among others, the date and number of official receipt issued in acknowledgement thereof, amount, purpose, date liquidated and other relevant information; Prepare and submit to the funding agency the Fund Utilization Report duly supported with adequate documents for liquidation of funds received; and Refund any unused balance in full settlement of liabilities (Due to NGAS).

Observe the limitations on the types of programs/projects/activities that may be funded under the 20% Development Funds to conform the requirements laid down under DILG-DBM Joint Circular DILG-DBM No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011, amending DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 dated September 20, 2005 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-138 dated December 2, 2010; and Submit the following audit requirement for further evaluation:

b.1 Program of Work with Detailed Plans and Specifications for rehabilitation/restoration of government buildings and structures, improvement, repair and maintenance of roads and other government facilities and other related construction projects funded out of financial assistance extended to Other LGUs and Individuals;

b.2  Pictures and Inspection Report of implemented projects;

b.3  List of items procured indicating its brief description, date acquired, cost and condition.

b.4 Copy  of  the Statement of Work Accomplished/Progress Report/Accomplishment Report rendered by the Municipal Engineer; and

b.5 Copy of Certificate of Completion and Certificate of Acceptance (if any) for 100% Accomplishment.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to effect adjustments for the period amounting to P845,598.00 from expense accounts 884, 878 and 821 (Miscellaneous Expenses, Donation and Repair and Maintenance) to account Due from Other LGUs/NGOs/POs and submit the necessary Liquidation Reports relative thereto for proper review and evaluation.

Observe strictly the period prescribed in the COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 89 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 pertinent to the liquidation of cash advances for travel; Instruct the Municipal Accountant to update the Subsidiary Ledger for travel indicating all detailed information, among others, are: date of grant, amount, purpose, date liquidated and other relevant information; and Demand from the concerned Officials and Employees the immediate submission of proper documentation as evidenced that the travel had been undertaken.

Conduct the immediate count of property, plant and equipment and submit the RPCPPE using the format shown in Appendix 49 of the Manual on NGAS for LGU’s, Volume II; and Reconcile the balances shown in the RPCPPE with the balances per accounting records and prepare the necessary adjustments, if any, for the difference.
